concept.title: Preprocessing Deep Dive
concept.shortdesc: DITA-OT preprocessing is a set of Ant-target steps that resolve keys, conrefs, chunking, and links before any format-specific transform runs.

section.title: Why preprocessing is structured as Ant targets
section.p: DITA-OT preprocessing is described as a set of steps that typically runs at the beginning of every transformation; each step corresponds to an Ant target, and the preprocess target calls the complete set.

ul.li: composable, declarative pipeline wiring (targets invoking Java or XSLT)

section.title: Common preprocessing responsibilities
section.p: A practical breakdown (you'll see these reflected in extension points and step names):

ol.li: copy-files / copy-image / copy-subsidiary: ensure resources are staged
gen-list: create file lists / job metadata
mapref / mappull / topicpull: map/topic "pull" logic (metadata, navtitle, etc.)
conref / conrefpush: resolve content reuse and push patterns
coderef: resolve code snippets pulled in
chunk: decide how topics are split (into pages or combined units)
maplink: generate relationship tables / link metadata
plus additional steps depending on pipeline and output

section.p: The development docs emphasize that these steps are targets in the Ant pipeline.

section.title: Where to hook preprocessing (best practice)
section.p: DITA-OT recommends using extension points that run before or after preprocessing, and warns against hooking into individual internal steps unless absolutely necessary.

ul.li: depend.preprocess.pre — run a target before preprocessing
depend.preprocess.post — run a target after preprocessing
your plug-in stays compatible across versions
you avoid being tied to fragile internal step order (which can change between releases)

section.title: Legacy step hooks (use sparingly)
section.p: If you must hook inside preprocessing, DITA-OT documents legacy extension points such as:
But there's a strong caution: internal step order can change, so these should only be used when truly necessary.
